record.field: Vikunja is a self-hosted task and project management platform that keeps your to-dos, plans and team work entirely under your control.

Vikunja is a self-hosted task and project management platform that keeps your to-dos, plans and team work entirely under your control.

It scales from a personal to-do list to collaborative team project planning without switching tools. Built on a Go backend with a Vue/TypeScript frontend, it offers multiple ways to visualize the same tasks and a full REST API for integration. It is a popular open-source alternative to Todoist, Trello and Microsoft To-Do.

Key features
  • Multiple task views: list, Kanban board, table and Gantt timeline
  • Nested projects with subtasks, labels, assignees, due dates and priorities
  • Recurring tasks with email reminders and progress tracking
  • Team collaboration: shared projects, task assignment and comments
  • Built-in migration from Todoist, Trello, Microsoft To-Do and others
  • Full REST API and Docker-based self-hosting
Best for

Individuals and teams who want to own their task data, replace SaaS to-do apps with a self-hosted alternative, and manage personal and collaborative projects from a single tool.

services: vikunja: image: vikunja/vikunja user: 0:0 environment: VIKUNJA_SERVICE_PUBLICURL: {{ vikunja_service_publicurl }} VIKUNJA_PUBLIC_PORT: ${VIKUNJA_PUBLIC_PORT} VIKUNJA_DATABASE_HOST: {{ vikunja_database_host }} VIKUNJA_DATABASE_PASSWORD: {{ vikunja_database_password }} VIKUNJA_DATABASE_TYPE: {{ vikunja_database_type }} VIKUNJA_DATABASE_USER: {{ vikunja_database_user }} VIKUNJA_DATABASE_DATABASE: {{ vikunja_database_database }} VIKUNJA_SERVICE_JWTSECRET: {{ vikunja_service_jwtsecret }} ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3456' volumes: - ./vikunja_files:/app/vikunja/files depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:17 environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ vikunja_database_password }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ vikunja_database_user }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ vikunja_database_database }} volumes: - ./vikunja_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready - -U - {{ vikunja_database_user }} - -d - {{ vikunja_database_database }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
